hover_copy.excess: Excess return = the fund minus IWF, both after fees. Raw scoreboard vs simply holding the index.
hover_copy.alpha: Alpha (beta-adjusted) = the fund minus a beta-scaled IWF position (beta 0.90). FCNTX runs less market risk than the index, so raw excess understates the manager; alpha compares against a passive position with the SAME market risk.

cohort.label: funds benchmarked to IWF
cohort.note: scored funds sharing FCNTX's closest passive alternative (IWF)

beta.readout: lower market sensitivity than 98% of the 158 IWF-benchmarked funds

tracking_error.readout: takes more benchmark-relative risk than 67% of the cohort

te_decomposition.label: PROTOTYPE — real inputs, ad-hoc method; production spec (te-decomposition-by-bet) is queued
te_decomposition.basis_note: bet betas measured on the market/FF6 basis (canonical_v1); the shipped feature will decompose tracking error vs the named passive alternative (IWF) — magnitudes/order may shift
te_decomposition.sleeve_note: roughly half of FCNTX's benchmark-relative risk is stock-specific (stock selection); half is factor/theme tilts
te_decomposition.diversifying_note: negative allocation = the bet moves against the rest of the book and REDUCES total tracking error

window.note: year-over-year holdings comparison from N-PORT filings; filings lag (~7 months stale at mock time) — always show both as-of dates

recent_changes.ranking_note: ordered by size of change (pp); ranking by tracking-error impact is pending the recent-changes-te-ranked backend spec
recent_changes.no_stock_rows_note: no qualifying single-position changes for FCNTX in this window; the stock filter tab should show an honest empty state

fund_family.grouping_note: family = fund adviser (N-CEN); SEC trust names are too granular (Fidelity spans ~45 trusts)
fund_family.rank_basis: AUM-weighted net value (bps/yr vs each fund's own closest passive alternative), since-inception basis, families with >=5 scored funds
fund_family.three_year_note: 3-year columns pending the profile-nav-series backend spec; do NOT invent 3y figures

ai_summary.label: SAMPLE AI COPY — v5.3 (fee corrected to canonical 0.74%/56 bps); production copy is LLM-generated with an in-code fact gate
ai_summary.rule: every number cited here MUST reappear in the page's visible evidence sections
paragraphs: Fidelity Contrafund is one of the largest actively managed US equity funds ($140.6B). Read against its closest passive alternative — the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF, 0.18% fee) — we estimate its net value at +10 bps/yr after fees: roughly breakeven. The fund's active management has historically earned back its fee, but not meaningfully more. Fees are middling: the 0.74% expense ratio works out to 56 bps/yr above simply holding IWF, slightly above the ~52 bps our fair-fee model estimates for its level of active work. A cheaper K share class (FCNKX, 0.32%) exists for eligible retirement accounts. Fidelity as a family ranks 4th of 116 fund families on AUM-weighted after-fee value added.

About half the portfolio behaves like the index; the other half is active bets. The defining ones today: a large underweight to Technology (25.9% held vs IWF's 53.8%), overweights to Communication Services (+13.0 pp) and Financial Services (+10.6 pp), and an outsized Meta position (15.7% vs 3.8% in IWF). Over the holdings era (2021–2025) the Communication Services tilt added +472 bps/yr of active return while the Technology underweight cost −355 bps/yr; over the last three years Meta alone contributed +3,098 bps vs IWF — the single biggest winner. The stock-picking record overall is statistically unproven: P(skill) 4%, information ratio −0.12 net of fees across 17 years — a positive gross selection estimate (+110 bps/yr) that cannot be distinguished from zero.

On risk: beta of 0.90 vs its passive baseline is lower than 98% of the 158 IWF-benchmarked funds, while tracking error of 4.8%/yr is higher than 67% of them. Over the year to September 2025 the manager cut Healthcare (−5.5 pp) and GLP-1/biotech exposure while adding to Semiconductors (+2.3 pp) and Communication Services (+2.3 pp). The main risk to the read is the structural Technology underweight: over the 2021–2025 window it cost −355 bps/yr as large-cap tech led. Manager names and tenure are not yet part of our sourced data; they will appear here once they are.

attribution_explorer.note: date-range control = two quarter-end selects over this grid + a 'since inception' button (returns-based read with an 'attribution covers the holdings era only' note). Full-window decomposition data = combined_decomposition key (real); member drill-down rows = attribution_full key (real 1Y/3Y/5Y). Custom sub-window recomputation is a FRONTEND INTERACTION MOCK ONLY — show the default full-window numbers, do not invent sub-window figures.

beta_tilt.label: Beta tilt (context bar — NOT part of the bet decomposition)
beta_tilt.note: PROTOTYPE estimate: (beta - 1) x IWF annualized return over the window. The bet decomposition below is measured AFTER beta adjustment (frozen active = fund - beta*baseline), so this bar must be shown separately and never summed into the bets+selection chain. Per-quarter integrated beta effect ships with spec attribution-factor-path-serving.

attribution_explorer.residual_explainer: Within the decomposition there is NO residual: bias+timing sum to each factor total by construction, and stock selection (idio) is defined as frozen active minus factor bets — so bets + selection = +93 bps/yr exactly. The separate +165 bps/yr reconciliation gap is frozen-vs-NAV: the decomposition is computed on quarterly FROZEN filed holdings GROSS of fees, while real NAV is NET and includes intra-quarter trading. Label it "fees + trading (not visible to holdings-based analysis)", never force it to zero.
attribution_explorer.basis_migration_note: The bets listed in Current Positioning and the factors attributed here currently come from two model generations — we are unifying them on one standardized factor set. Until then, several Positioning bets are attributed inside an overlapping factor here rather than on their own line.

risk_explainers.beta: Beta measures how much the fund moves when its index (IWF) moves. Beta 1.00 = moves one-for-one. FCNTX's 0.90 means a 10% IWF move typically moves the fund about 9% — it holds LESS market risk than the index.
risk_explainers.tracking_error: Tracking error measures how differently the fund behaves from its index, in %/yr. Zero = an index hugger. FCNTX's 4.8%/yr means its returns typically land within ±4.8 points of the (beta-adjusted) index in a normal year — that spread is what active management buys you, for better or worse.
risk_explainers.beta_tilt_plain: The fund holds less market risk than its index (beta 0.90 vs 1.00). In a market that rose ~16%/yr over this window, holding 10% less of it cost about 150 bps/yr of raw relative return. This is a POSITIONING choice, not a stock-picking result — so we measure all the bets AFTER removing it, and show it here as its own line.

holdings_full.basis: classified US-equity positions from the N-PORT filing (280 rows; the fund also holds cash and unclassified/non-US sleeves)

top10_vs_iwf.basis_note: fund and IWF weights from the same-date holdings snapshots (same source family) — may differ ~1pp from the Exposure X-Ray classification

rows.note: not held by IWF (Russell 1000 Growth excludes it)

positioning_bet_bridges.note: real cluster mapping from the risk model (holdings_exposure_path cluster_id)

bridges.bridge: not separately attributed today — folds into stock selection
not separately attributed today — folds into stock selection
not separately attributed today — folds into stock selection
